content.text: MASTER SERVICES AGREEMENT
Draft v2 — For Review

This Master Services Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of March 1, 2026 (the "Effective Date"), by and between Apex Dynamics Inc., a Delaware corporation ("Client"), and Stratos Cloud Solutions LLC, a Virginia limited liability company ("Provider").
RECITALS
WHEREAS, Client desires to engage Provider to deliver managed cloud infrastructure services; and
WHEREAS, Provider has the expertise, personnel, and resources necessary to perform such services;
NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants and agreements set forth herein, the parties agree as follows:
Article 1 — Definitions

"Confidential Information" means any non-public information disclosed by either party to the other, whether in written, oral, or electronic form, that is designated as confidential or that a reasonable person would understand to be confidential given the nature of the information and circumstances of disclosure.
"Services" means the managed cloud infrastructure services described in Article 2 and any Statements of Work executed under this Agreement.
"Deliverables" means all work product, reports, documentation, and materials produced by Provider in connection with the Services.
Article 2 — Scope of Services

2.1  Provider shall deliver the following managed cloud infrastructure services: (a) provisioning, configuration, and management of cloud compute, storage, and networking resources on AWS and Azure; (b) 24/7 monitoring, incident detection, and response; (c) monthly capacity planning and optimization reports; (d) disaster recovery architecture with agreed RPO and RTO targets; and (e) quarterly business reviews.
2.2  The parties may supplement this Agreement with one or more Statements of Work, each of which shall be governed by the terms of this Agreement.
Article 3 — Term and Renewal
3.1  This Agreement commences on the Effective Date and continues for a period of three (3) years (the "Initial Term").

3.2  Upon expiration of the Initial Term, this Agreement shall automatically renew for successive one (1) year periods (each a "Renewal Term") unless either party provides written notice of non-renewal at least sixty (60) days prior to the end of the then-current term.
Article 4 — Fees and Payment
4.1  Client shall pay Provider a monthly service fee of Forty-Five Thousand Dollars ($45,000.00), payable in arrears.
4.2  Invoices are due and payable within forty-five (45) days of receipt ("Net 45"). Late payments shall accrue interest at the lesser of 1.5% per month or the maximum rate permitted by law.
4.3  Provider may increase fees upon sixty (60) days' written notice, provided that any increase shall not exceed 5% per annum.
Article 5 — Service Level Agreement
5.1  Provider guarantees a monthly uptime of 99.9% for all production environments.

5.2  In the event Provider fails to meet the uptime target, Client shall be entitled to service credits equal to 5% of the monthly fee for each 0.05% shortfall below the target, up to a maximum credit of 20% of the monthly fee.
5.3  Service credits are Client's sole and exclusive remedy for Provider's failure to meet the uptime commitment set forth in Section 5.1.
Article 6 — Confidentiality and Data Protection
6.1  Each party agrees to hold the other party's Confidential Information in strict confidence and not to disclose it to any third party without prior written consent, except as required by law.
6.2  Provider shall encrypt all Client data at rest using AES-256 encryption and in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher.
6.3  Provider shall implement commercially reasonable security controls consistent with industry best practices for cloud service providers.
6.4  Provider shall not transfer Client data outside the continental United States without prior written consent.
Article 7 — Intellectual Property

7.1  Provider shall retain all right, title, and interest in and to any tools, scripts, configurations, and methodologies developed in connection with the Services, whether pre-existing or newly created. Client is granted a non-exclusive, perpetual license to use such materials solely in connection with Client's internal operations.
7.2  Client retains all right, title, and interest in Client's pre-existing intellectual property and data.
Article 8 — Limitation of Liability
8.1  EXCEPT FOR BREACHES OF ARTICLE 6 (CONFIDENTIALITY), NEITHER PARTY'S AGGREGATE LIABILITY UNDER THIS AGREEMENT SHALL EXCEED AN AMOUNT EQUAL TO SIX (6) MONTHS OF FEES PAID OR PAYABLE HEREUNDER.
8.2  IN NO EVENT SHALL EITHER PARTY BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, REGARDLESS OF THE CAUSE OF ACTION OR THEORY OF LIABILITY.
Article 9 — Termination
9.1  Either party may terminate this Agreement for convenience by providing sixty (60) days' prior written notice to the other party.
9.2  Either party may terminate this Agreement for cause upon thirty (30) days' written notice if the other party commits a material breach and fails to cure such breach within the notice period.

9.3  Upon termination, Provider shall (a) cease performance of the Services, (b) return or destroy all Client Confidential Information, and (c) cooperate in good faith with any transition activities for a period of up to ninety (90) days, at Client's expense.
Article 10 — Governing Law and Dispute Resolution
10.1  This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York, without regard to its conflict of laws principles.

10.2  Any dispute arising under this Agreement shall first be submitted to good-faith mediation. If mediation fails to resolve the dispute within sixty (60) days, either party may initiate binding arbitration in New York, New York under the rules of the American Arbitration Association.
Article 11 — General Provisions

11.1  Entire Agreement.  This Agreement, together with any Statements of Work and exhibits, constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes all prior or contemporaneous understandings.
11.2  Amendment.  This Agreement may be amended only by a written instrument signed by both parties.
11.3  Waiver.  The failure of either party to enforce any right under this Agreement shall not constitute a waiver of that right.
11.4  Severability.  If any provision of this Agreement is held to be invalid, the remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect.
11.5  Notices.  All notices under this Agreement shall be in writing and delivered by certified mail, overnight courier, or email with confirmed receipt to the addresses set forth in this Agreement.

11.6  Assignment.  Neither party may assign this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other party, except in connection with a merger, acquisition, or sale of substantially all of the assigning party's assets.
IN WITNESS WHEREOF
The parties have executed this Master Services Agreement as of the Effective Date first written above.
APEX DYNAMICS INC.
Name: Catherine A. Morales
Title: Chief Operating Officer
STRATOS CLOUD SOLUTIONS LLC
Name: David R. Thornton
Title: Managing Director
EXECUTED TERM SHEET
Managed Cloud Infrastructure Services
CONFIDENTIAL
1. Parties
Client: Apex Dynamics Inc., a Delaware corporation with its principal office at 200 Innovation Drive, Suite 800, Wilmington, DE 19801 ("Client" or "Apex").
Provider: Stratos Cloud Solutions LLC, a Virginia limited liability company with its principal office at 1455 Reston Parkway, Suite 320, Reston, VA 20190 ("Provider" or "Stratos").
2. Effective Date
March 1, 2026.
3. Scope of Services

Provider shall deliver managed cloud infrastructure services to Client, including: (a) provisioning, configuration, and management of cloud compute, storage, and networking resources on AWS and Azure; (b) 24/7 monitoring, incident detection, and response; (c) monthly capacity planning and optimization reports; (d) disaster recovery architecture with a Recovery Point Objective (RPO) of 1 hour and Recovery Time Objective (RTO) of 4 hours; and (e) quarterly business reviews with Client's IT leadership team.
4. Term
Three (3) years from the Effective Date, expiring February 28, 2029, unless earlier terminated in accordance with Section 10.
5. Fees and Payment
Monthly Service Fee: Forty-Seven Thousand Five Hundred Dollars ($47,500.00) per month, payable in arrears.
Payment Terms: Net Thirty (30) days from receipt of a valid invoice. Late payments shall accrue interest at 1.5% per month.
6. Service Level Agreement

Provider guarantees a monthly uptime of 99.95% for all production environments. Failure to meet this target entitles Client to service credits equal to 5% of the monthly fee for each 0.01% below 99.95%, up to a maximum credit of 30% of the monthly fee.
7. Data Handling and Compliance

Provider shall maintain SOC 2 Type II certification throughout the Term and provide annual audit reports to Client within thirty (30) days of report issuance. Provider shall encrypt all Client data at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.2+). Provider shall not transfer Client data outside the continental United States without prior written consent.
8. Intellectual Property

All custom configurations, scripts, automation tooling, and derivative works created by Provider specifically for Client under this engagement ("Custom Work Product") shall be the exclusive property of Client. Provider retains ownership of its pre-existing tools, frameworks, and methodologies.
9. Limitation of Liability

Each party's aggregate liability under this agreement shall not exceed an amount equal to twelve (12) months of fees paid or payable under this Term Sheet (the "Liability Cap"). This cap does not apply to breaches of confidentiality obligations, willful misconduct, or indemnification obligations arising from third-party IP claims.
10. Termination

Either party may terminate this agreement for convenience by providing ninety (90) days' prior written notice to the other party. Either party may terminate for cause upon thirty (30) days' written notice if the other party materially breaches this agreement and fails to cure such breach within the notice period.
11. Governing Law and Dispute Resolution

This agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware, without regard to its conflict of laws principles. Any dispute arising under this agreement shall be resolved by binding arbitration in Wilmington, Delaware under the rules of the American Arbitration Association.
12. Signatures
IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed this Term Sheet as of the Effective Date.
APEX DYNAMICS INC.
By: /s/ Catherine A. Morales
Name: Catherine A. Morales
Title: Chief Operating Officer
Date: March 1, 2026
STRATOS CLOUD SOLUTIONS LLC
By: /s/ David R. Thornton
Name: David R. Thornton
Title: Managing Director
Date: March 1, 2026
CONTRACT REVIEW MEMORANDUM
TO:       Catherine A. Morales, COO — Apex Dynamics Inc.
FROM:     Outside Counsel — Contract Review Team
DATE:     March 16, 2026
RE:       Review of Draft MSA v2 — Stratos Cloud Solutions LLC
1. Executive Summary

We have completed a line-by-line comparison of the executed Term Sheet dated March 1, 2026, against Draft MSA v2 circulated by Stratos Cloud Solutions LLC. Our review identified nine (9) discrepancies between the two documents, of which three are classified as Critical, three as High, two as Medium, and one as Low severity. The most significant issues involve the monthly service fee, intellectual property ownership, and the liability cap — all of which deviate materially from the agreed commercial terms. We strongly recommend that these discrepancies be resolved before the MSA is executed.
2. Discrepancy Summary Table
3. Detailed Analysis
3.1  Monthly Service Fee (Article 4.1) — CRITICAL

The Term Sheet sets the monthly service fee at $47,500.00. The draft MSA states $45,000.00, a $2,500 per-month shortfall that amounts to $90,000 over the three-year term. This is almost certainly a drafting error, but if left uncorrected it would bind Client to a higher payment than negotiated or create ambiguity that Provider could exploit.
Recommended language:
"Client shall pay Provider a monthly service fee of Forty-Seven Thousand Five Hundred Dollars ($47,500.00), payable in arrears."
3.2  Payment Terms (Article 4.2) — CRITICAL

The Term Sheet specifies Net 30 payment terms. The draft MSA extends this to Net 45. While a longer payment window may appear to benefit Client, the inconsistency creates a contract-interpretation risk. More importantly, the late-payment interest provision could trigger unexpectedly if Client's accounts payable processes are calibrated to the Net 30 cycle agreed in the Term Sheet.
Recommended language:
"Invoices are due and payable within thirty (30) days of receipt ("Net 30"). Late payments shall accrue interest at 1.5% per month."
3.3  SLA Uptime Target (Article 5.1) — HIGH

The Term Sheet guarantees 99.95% monthly uptime. The draft MSA reduces this to 99.9%. The difference of 0.05% translates to approximately 22 additional minutes of permissible downtime per month. For mission-critical cloud infrastructure, this dilution of the uptime commitment materially weakens Client's operational protections. Additionally, the service-credit formula in Section 5.2 of the MSA caps credits at 20% of the monthly fee, whereas the Term Sheet allows up to 30%.
Recommended language:

"Provider guarantees a monthly uptime of 99.95% for all production environments. Service credits shall equal 5% of the monthly fee for each 0.01% below 99.95%, up to a maximum credit of 30% of the monthly fee."
3.4  SOC 2 Type II Compliance (Article 6) — HIGH

The Term Sheet explicitly requires Provider to maintain SOC 2 Type II certification and furnish annual audit reports. The draft MSA contains no reference to SOC 2 compliance; Section 6.3 instead uses vague "commercially reasonable security controls" language. This omission exposes Client to significant compliance risk, particularly if Client is subject to regulatory requirements that depend on vendor SOC 2 attestation.
Recommended language:

"Provider shall maintain SOC 2 Type II certification throughout the Term and shall provide Client with a copy of its annual SOC 2 Type II audit report within thirty (30) days of the report's issuance. Provider shall promptly notify Client of any material findings or qualifications in such report."
3.5  Intellectual Property Ownership (Article 7.1) — CRITICAL

This is the most consequential discrepancy. The Term Sheet provides that Client owns all custom work product. The draft MSA reverses this entirely: Provider retains ownership of all materials (including newly created work), granting Client only a non-exclusive license. This would leave Client without ownership of custom configurations and automation built specifically for its environment, creating vendor lock-in and jeopardizing Client's ability to transition to another provider.
Recommended language:

"All custom configurations, scripts, automation tooling, and derivative works created by Provider specifically for Client under this Agreement ("Custom Work Product") shall be the exclusive property of Client. Provider retains ownership of its pre-existing tools, frameworks, and methodologies."
3.6  Liability Cap (Article 8.1) — HIGH

The Term Sheet sets the liability cap at twelve (12) months of fees ($570,000). The draft MSA reduces this to six (6) months ($285,000 based on the MSA's lower fee figure, or $270,000 using the correct fee). Halving the liability cap substantially limits Client's recourse in the event of a significant service failure or data breach. The Term Sheet also carves out confidentiality breaches, willful misconduct, and third-party IP claims from the cap — the MSA only carves out confidentiality.
Recommended language:

"Each party's aggregate liability under this Agreement shall not exceed twelve (12) months of fees paid or payable hereunder. This cap shall not apply to breaches of confidentiality obligations, willful misconduct, or indemnification obligations arising from third-party intellectual property claims."
3.7  Termination Notice Period (Article 9.1) — MEDIUM

The Term Sheet requires 90 days' notice for termination for convenience. The draft MSA shortens this to 60 days. While a shorter notice period gives both parties more flexibility, it reduces Client's transition runway if Provider terminates. Given the complexity of cloud infrastructure migration, 90 days is more appropriate.
Recommended language:
"Either party may terminate this Agreement for convenience by providing ninety (90) days' prior written notice to the other party."
3.8  Governing Law (Article 10) — MEDIUM

The Term Sheet designates Delaware as the governing jurisdiction, consistent with Client's state of incorporation. The draft MSA substitutes New York. While both jurisdictions have well-developed commercial law, the change was not negotiated and would require Client to litigate or arbitrate disputes in a different forum. This should be corrected to maintain consistency with the Term Sheet and Client's corporate nexus.
Recommended language:

"This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Delaware. Any dispute shall be resolved by binding arbitration in Wilmington, Delaware under the rules of the American Arbitration Association."
3.9  Auto-Renewal Clause (Article 3.2) — LOW

The draft MSA introduces an automatic renewal provision for successive one-year terms. The Term Sheet contains no such provision — it contemplates a fixed three-year term. While auto-renewal clauses are common in services agreements, this term was not discussed or agreed upon. Client should either delete the clause entirely or negotiate acceptable renewal terms with adequate notice provisions.
Recommended action:
Delete Section 3.2 in its entirety, or revise to require mutual written agreement to renew.
4. Risk Assessment
Overall Risk Rating: HIGH

The draft MSA contains multiple material deviations from the executed Term Sheet. Three discrepancies — the monthly fee, IP ownership, and payment terms — are classified as Critical because they directly affect Client's financial exposure and proprietary rights. The cumulative effect of all nine discrepancies significantly shifts the risk balance in Provider's favor. The MSA should not be executed in its current form.

Of particular concern is the IP ownership reversal (Section 7.1). If executed as drafted, Client would lose ownership of all custom work product developed during the engagement, creating substantial vendor lock-in and undermining Client's negotiating position in any future re-procurement.
5. Recommended Next Steps
We recommend the following prioritized actions:
1.  Immediate Redline — Prepare a marked-up version of the MSA correcting all nine discrepancies identified above. Priority should be given to the three Critical items (fee, payment terms, IP).

2.  Provider Discussion — Schedule a call with Stratos's legal team within the next five business days to review the redline and confirm whether the discrepancies were drafting errors or intentional deviations.
3.  SOC 2 Verification — Request a copy of Provider's current SOC 2 Type II report before executing the MSA to confirm certification status.
4.  Auto-Renewal Negotiation — Determine Client's position on auto-renewal and, if acceptable in principle, negotiate a mutual opt-in renewal mechanism with at least 90 days' advance notice.
5.  Final Review — Once the redline is accepted, conduct a final review of the revised MSA to ensure all changes have been accurately incorporated before routing for signature.
Please do not hesitate to contact us if you have questions or wish to discuss any of the above findings in greater detail.

rows: Rewrite to assign IP to Client

content.text: CONTRACT REVIEW MEMORANDUM
TO: Catherine A. Morales, Chief Operating Officer, Apex Dynamics Inc.
FROM: Legal Department
DATE: [Current Date]
RE: Contract Review — Draft Master Services Agreement v2 with Stratos Cloud Solutions LLC
EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

Our review of the draft Master Services Agreement (v2) reveals significant deviations from the executed Term Sheet dated March 1, 2026. We identified eleven (11) material discrepancies across key commercial terms, service commitments, and legal provisions. Three discrepancies are rated as Critical, affecting pricing, service levels, and intellectual property rights. Four are rated as High priority, impacting liability protections and dispute resolution mechanisms. We recommend rejecting the current draft and providing a comprehensive markup addressing all identified issues before proceeding with negotiations.
DISCREPANCY ANALYSIS TABLE
DETAILED ANALYSIS
1. Critical Discrepancies
Monthly Service Fee (Article 4.1)

The draft MSA reduces the monthly fee from $47,500 to $45,000, representing a $2,500 monthly decrease or $90,000 over the three-year term. This material financial deviation from the executed Term Sheet is unacceptable and suggests potential bad faith in the drafting process.
Intellectual Property Rights (Article 7.1)

The Term Sheet clearly grants Client ownership of all "Custom Work Product" created specifically for this engagement. The draft MSA reverses this critical provision, allowing Provider to retain ownership while granting only a license to Client. This fundamentally alters the value proposition and could create dependencies that limit Client's operational flexibility.
Service Level Agreement (Article 5)

The draft MSA weakens the uptime guarantee from 99.95% to 99.9%, effectively allowing an additional 36 minutes of downtime per month. Additionally, the credit structure is diluted (credits trigger at 0.05% intervals instead of 0.01%) and the maximum credit is reduced from 30% to 20%.
2. High Priority Discrepancies
Payment Terms (Article 4.2)
Extending payment terms from Net 30 to Net 45 days provides Provider with a significant cash flow advantage at Client's expense. This 50% extension contradicts the agreed commercial terms.
Limitation of Liability (Article 8.1)
Reducing the liability cap from 12 months to 6 months of fees significantly limits Client's recovery options in case of Provider breach, reducing the cap from approximately $570,000 to $270,000.
Governing Law and Dispute Resolution (Articles 10.1-10.2)

The change from Delaware law to New York law and the addition of mandatory mediation creates procedural delays and shifts the legal framework away from Delaware's well-established corporate law precedents.
3. Medium Priority Discrepancies
Termination Notice (Article 9.1)
Reducing the termination notice period from 90 to 60 days may complicate transition planning and vendor replacement efforts.
Compliance Requirements (Article 6.3)
The omission of the SOC 2 Type II certification requirement removes an important security control that was specifically negotiated in the Term Sheet.
RISK ASSESSMENT
Overall Risk Rating: HIGH
The cumulative impact of these discrepancies creates substantial financial, operational, and legal risks:
**Financial Impact:** $90,000 direct cost increase plus extended payment terms
**Operational Risk:** Weakened SLAs and reduced oversight mechanisms
**Legal Risk:** Diminished liability protections and unfavorable dispute resolution
**Strategic Risk:** Loss of intellectual property rights in custom solutions
RECOMMENDED NEXT STEPS
Immediate Actions (Within 5 Business Days)
**Reject Current Draft:** Formally notify Stratos that the draft MSA contains material deviations from the executed Term Sheet and is unacceptable in its current form.
**Provide Marked-Up Version:** Deliver a comprehensive redline of the draft MSA incorporating all corrections identified in this memo.
Short-Term Actions (Within 2 Weeks)
**Negotiate Resolution:** Schedule a meeting with Stratos leadership to discuss the discrepancies and obtain their commitment to honor the original Term Sheet terms.
**Document Understanding:** Obtain written acknowledgment from Stratos that any final MSA must be consistent with the executed Term Sheet provisions.
Medium-Term Actions (Within 30 Days)
**Finalize Documentation:** Execute a revised MSA that accurately reflects all Term Sheet commitments.
**Implementation Planning:** Begin service transition activities only after acceptable contract terms are secured.
Contingency Planning
**Alternative Vendors:** Given the significant deviations in this draft, consider identifying backup service providers to maintain negotiating leverage and ensure business continuity.
CONFIDENTIAL AND PRIVILEGED
This memorandum contains attorney work product and is intended solely for the use of Apex Dynamics Inc. management in connection with the referenced transaction.

rows: Correct to $47,500 per executed Term Sheet
Remove auto-renewal; specify fixed term ending Feb. 28, 2029
Revert to Net 30 day payment terms
99.95% uptime guarantee; 5% credit per 0.01% shortfall; max 30% credit
99.9% uptime guarantee; 5% credit per 0.05% shortfall; max 20% credit
Restore 99.95% SLA with original credit structure
Client owns all Custom Work Product
Provider retains all tools/configurations; grants license to Client
Restore Client ownership of Custom Work Product
Mediation first, then arbitration in New York
Remove mediation requirement; arbitration in Wilmington, DE
Add SOC 2 Type II certification requirement
Specify RPO of 1 hour and RTO of 4 hours
